scenarios.title: Battery capacity degradation due to normal aging
scenarios.description: The iPhone's battery capacity has depleted by approximately 45% due to two years of regular use without any accidental damage or defects.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or that are otherwise due to normal aging of the product.
Battery capacity coverage applies only when capacity has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications.

scenarios.reasoning: Battery degradation under 50% capacity loss is considered normal aging and wear, explicitly excluded under section 4.2(vii). Since the battery has only depleted 45%, this does not meet the minimum threshold for coverage under section 3.1, resulting in denial without a service fee.
